title: Python Error Handling Review
prompt:
Review this Python function and suggest improvements:

def get_user(id):
    result = db.query(f"SELECT * FROM users WHERE id = {id}")
    return result[0]
guidelines: Look for security vulnerabilities (SQL injection, etc.)
Check if the review identifies all major issues
Assess whether the suggested fix is production-ready

responses.label: Response A
responses.text:
There are several issues with this function:

**1. SQL Injection Vulnerability (Critical)**
Using f-strings for SQL queries allows SQL injection attacks. Use parameterized queries instead.

**2. No Error Handling**
If the query returns no results, `result[0]` will raise an IndexError.

**3. Using SELECT ***
This fetches unnecessary columns and exposes your schema.

**Improved version:**
```python
def get_user(user_id: int) -> dict | None:
    try:
        result = db.query(
            "SELECT id, name, email FROM users WHERE id = %s",
            (user_id,)
        )
        return result[0] if result else None
    except DatabaseError as e:
        logger.error(f"Failed to fetch user {user_id}: {e}")
        raise
```

responses.label: Response B

dimensions.name: Security Awareness
dimensions.description: Does the review identify security issues?

dimensions.name: Fix Quality
dimensions.description: Is the suggested fix correct and complete?

dimensions.name: Educational Value
dimensions.description: Does it help the developer learn?

guidelines: Check if the optimization advice is correct

Consider if the explanation is clear for intermediate React developers

responses.label: Response A
Response B

title: Git Commit Message Quality
prompt: A developer fixed a bug where users were logged out when switching tabs due to a race condition in the token refresh logic. Rank these commit messages from best to worst.
guidelines: Good commit messages explain WHY, not just WHAT
Should follow conventional commit format ideally
Should help future developers understand the change

responses.label: Message A
responses.text:
fix: prevent session loss on tab switch by serializing token refresh

The token refresh endpoint was being called concurrently when multiple
tabs became active simultaneously, causing a race condition where the
first response would invalidate the token used by the second request.

Added a mutex lock around the refresh flow to ensure only one refresh
executes at a time. Other tabs wait for the lock and reuse the new token.

Fixes #847

responses.label: Message C
responses.text:
Fix token refresh race condition

Users were getting logged out when switching between tabs because
concurrent refresh requests invalidated each other's tokens.

dimensions.name: Descriptiveness
dimensions.description: Does it explain what changed and why?

dimensions.name: Conciseness
dimensions.description: Is it appropriately brief without losing meaning?

dimensions.name: Actionability
dimensions.description: Would this help a future developer understand the context?

title: API Design Feedback
prompt: Rate this response to: "Design a REST API for a simple todo application with users."
guidelines: Evaluate RESTful design principles (proper HTTP methods, status codes, resource naming)
Assess whether the response covers pagination, filtering, error responses

responses.label: API Design Response
responses.text:
## Todo API Design

### Authentication
- POST /auth/register — Create account
- POST /auth/login — Get JWT token
- POST /auth/refresh — Refresh token

### Users
- GET /users/me — Get current user profile
- PATCH /users/me — Update profile

### Todos
- GET /todos?status=pending&page=1&limit=20 — List user's todos (paginated)
- POST /todos — Create todo
- GET /todos/:id — Get single todo
- PATCH /todos/:id — Update todo
- DELETE /todos/:id — Delete todo

### Response Format
```json
{
  "data": { ... },
  "meta": {
    "page": 1,
    "limit": 20,
    "total": 45
  }
}
```

### Error Format
```json
{
  "error": {
    "code": "VALIDATION_ERROR",
    "message": "Title is required",
    "details": [...]
  }
}
```

### Status Codes
- 200: Success
- 201: Created
- 204: Deleted (no content)
- 400: Validation error
- 401: Unauthorized
- 403: Forbidden
- 404: Not found
- 429: Rate limited

dimensions.name: Completeness
dimensions.description: Covers all necessary endpoints and concerns?

dimensions.name: Production Readiness
dimensions.description: Would this work in a real application?
